product_overview: Ishara is a communication platform designed for deaf/mute users and interpreters, facilitating seamless video calls, session booking, and real-time interaction through an accessible and intuitive interface leveraging PeerJS for video communication.
core_goals: Provide reliable and real-time video calling between deaf/mute users and interpreters using PeerJS.
Enable efficient session booking for immediate or scheduled interpreting services.
Allow interpreters to manage session requests (accept or reject) easily.
Allow interpreters to set their weekly availability to optimize session scheduling.
Implement a robust authentication system supporting email/password and Google sign-in.
Deliver in-app and push notifications for session updates and reminders.
Provide rating and feedback mechanisms for interpreters after session completion.
Offer role-based dashboards tailored for deaf/mute users and interpreters to manage their activities.
key_features: Authentication via email/password and Google sign-in for secure user access.
Session booking system to book immediate or scheduled interpreting sessions.
PeerJS-based real-time video and audio calls with screen sharing and call controls (mute, camera toggle, end call).
Session requests management for interpreters to accept or reject bookings.
Availability management for interpreters to set and update weekly schedules.
In-app and push notification system to keep users informed of session updates.
Rating system allowing users to rate interpreters and provide feedback post-session.
Role-specific dashboards for deaf/mute users and interpreters to view and manage sessions.
Comprehensive error handling and automatic cleanup during video calls.
Migration from Agora to PeerJS for simplified signaling, free usage, and better reliability.
user_flow_summary: User signs up or logs in using email/password or Google sign-in.
Deaf/mute user books an immediate or scheduled session with an interpreter.
Interpreter receives session requests and accepts or rejects them.
Upon confirmation, both parties join the video call room; PeerJS handles call setup using peer IDs.
During the call, users can mute/unmute audio, toggle the camera, and share their screen.
After the session ends, the user rates the interpreter and optionally provides feedback.
Interpreters manage their availability schedule through the availability interface.
Users receive notifications about session statuses and relevant updates through the notification center.
Users access their role-based dashboards to manage sessions and view activity summaries.
validation_criteria: User authentication and authorization work seamlessly with email/password and Google sign-in.
Session booking functions correctly, allowing immediate and scheduled sessions.
Interpreters can accept or reject session requests, with updates reflected in real-time.
Video calls connect successfully using PeerJS with stable audio and video streams.
Call controls including mute, camera toggle, screen sharing, and call termination function as intended.
Availability schedules are correctly saved and retrieved for interpreters.
Notifications are received timely and accurately reflect session changes.
Ratings and feedback for sessions are recorded and retrievable.
Dashboards display accurate information tailored to the user role.
System handles errors gracefully, with logs and user feedback for issues during video calls.
Migration from Agora to PeerJS removes dependency on Agora SDK and enables stable peer-to-peer calls with simplified signaling.
